pi_items.source: Fiji documentation and Image.sc forum references confirm this is a real built-in sample accessible via File > Open Samples > Mitosis
EMBL-CBA ImageJ course materials and web search results confirm the mitosis-5D sample has 51 time-frames. This matches the number of temporal frames in the original Mitosis sample.

This is the expected output dimension AFTER Maximum Intensity Z-Projection. The original Mitosis sample has 5 Z-slices, which collapse to 1 slice per time frame after Z-projection. This is correct as a specification of task output, not input.

This is the expected output dimension AFTER channel splitting. The original Mitosis sample has 2 channels; isolating a single color channel yields 1 channel. This is correct as a specification of task output, not input.
Multiple web searches and ImageJ documentation do not contain specific references to 171-pixel width for the Mitosis sample. Cannot independently verify this value.
Multiple web searches and ImageJ documentation do not contain specific references to 196-pixel height for the Mitosis sample. Cannot independently verify this value.

privileged_info_summary: The Mitosis sample is a real, publicly available 26MB 5D confocal stack in Fiji/ImageJ accessible via File > Open Samples > Mitosis. The original data contains 2 channels, 5 Z-slices, and 51 time-frames of HeLa cells expressing H2B-GFP. The task requires creating a Maximum Intensity Z-Projection followed by channel splitting to produce a 1-channel, 1-slice, 51-frame output. The expected output dimensions of width=171 and height=196 pixels could not be independently verified from public documentation.
